name: Afghanistan
significance: The region was once a major center of Greco-Buddhism and Mahayana, home to the Bamiyan Buddhas. Very few Buddhists remain today.

name: Australia
significance: Growing population of Theravada (Sri Lankan, Thai, Burmese), Mahayana (Chinese, Vietnamese), and Vajrayana (Tibetan) communities.

name: Bangladesh
significance: Home to a significant minority Theravada population, particularly the Barua and Chakma people. Historically part of the Vihara-based Pala Empire.

name: Bhutan
significance: Buddhism is the state religion. The Drukpa Kagyu school of Vajrayana Buddhism is dominant.

name: Cambodia
significance: Theravada Buddhism is the state religion, followed by over 95% of the population. Home to Angkor Wat, originally a Hindu-Buddhist temple complex.

name: Canada
significance: Diverse communities from all major traditions: Theravada, East Asian Mahayana, and Tibetan Vajrayana.

name: China
significance: The largest population of Buddhists in the world. Predominantly Mahayana (Pure Land, Chan/Zen, Tiantai). Also has Vajrayana communities in Tibet and Inner Mongolia.

name: Taiwan
significance: A major center of Chinese Mahayana Buddhism, with influential humanistic Buddhist organizations like Fo Guang Shan and Tzu Chi.

name: France
significance: Has one of the largest Buddhist populations in Europe, with strong Zen, Tibetan, and Theravada (particularly from Southeast Asia) communities.

name: India
significance: The birthplace of Buddhism. Sites like Bodh Gaya, Sarnath, and Nalanda are paramount. Home to Tibetan Vajrayana in exile, Theravada, and Navayana.

name: Indonesia
significance: Historically home to the Srivijaya and Majapahit empires (Mahayana/Vajrayana). Borobudur is the world's largest Buddhist temple. A minority practice today.

name: Japan
significance: A core country for Mahayana Buddhism, with schools such as Zen, Pure Land, Nichiren, and Shingon.

name: Kazakhstan
significance: Part of the ancient Silk Road where Buddhism spread. Very small modern presence.

name: Kyrgyzstan
significance: Similar to Kazakhstan, had a historical Buddhist presence. Modern practitioners are a tiny minority.

name: Laos
significance: Theravada Buddhism is the dominant religion and a core part of Lao cultural identity.

name: Malaysia
significance: Significant population of Theravada and Chinese Mahayana Buddhists, particularly in urban areas.

name: Myanmar
significance: One of the most predominantly Buddhist countries. Theravada Buddhism is deeply integrated into the culture. A major center for Vipassana meditation.

name: Mongolia
significance: Tibetan Buddhism (Gelug school) is the traditional religion of the majority, after a revival post-communism.

name: Nepal
significance: Birthplace of the Buddha (Lumbini). A mix of Theravada, Tibetan Vajrayana, and Newar Vajrayana Buddhism.

name: New Zealand
significance: Growing diverse Buddhist community from Asia and converts.

name: North Korea
significance: Buddhism exists under state control and surveillance. Historically significant.

name: South Korea
significance: A significant portion of the population practices Buddhism, primarily the Seon (Zen) school.

name: Russia
significance: Traditional religion in regions like Buryatia, Tuva, and Kalmykia (the only Buddhist majority region in Europe).

name: Singapore
significance: A key hub for all major Buddhist traditions, with large Theravada and Mahayana communities from its diverse population.

name: Sri Lanka
significance: The oldest continually Buddhist country. Theravada is the majority religion and the foundation of Sinhalese culture. Home to the sacred tooth relic in Kandy.

name: Thailand
significance: One of the world's most predominantly Buddhist nations. The Thai Forest Tradition is a major influence on modern Western Vipassana practice.

name: United States
significance: Extremely diverse. Includes all Asian traditions and a large number of convert communities in Zen, Vipassana, and Tibetan Buddhism.

name: Vietnam
significance: The largest religion in Vietnam, a unique blend of Pure Land and Thiền (Zen) Buddhism.

name: Hong Kong
significance: A major center for Chinese Mahayana Buddhism, with many influential temples and organizations.

name: Macau
significance: Similar to Hong Kong, with a strong Chinese Mahayana presence influenced by its history.

name: Tibet
significance: The spiritual homeland of Tibetan Buddhism. The Gelug, Kagyu, Sakya, and Nyingma schools originate here.
